project: Factory Run Records and Event Timeline
description: Add a durable file-backed factory store under Hal's global config directory, with persisted run records, append-only event timelines, JSON CLI read surfaces, contract documentation, examples, and tests while leaving existing .hal runtime state contracts unchanged.

userStories.title: Define factory run and event schemas
userStories.description: As a Hal maintainer, I want centralized run and event types so that factory state has a stable internal and JSON representation.
acceptanceCriteria: Add an internal/factory package with exported run record and event timeline types.

Run records include run ID, status, source metadata, repository path, repository remote, branch name, base branch, optional sandbox name, current pipeline step, timestamps, artifact references, and optional failure summary.
Event records include an event ID or sequence field, run ID, event type, timestamp, message or summary, and optional structured metadata.
JSON tags are stable camelCase names.
Marshal and unmarshal tests assert the JSON keys for run records, artifact references, failure summaries, and events.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Resolve global factory store paths
userStories.description: As a Hal maintainer, I want the factory store rooted under Hal's global config directory so that run records are separate from per-project .hal state.
acceptanceCriteria: Factory store path resolution reuses the existing global config directory precedence.
Factory store directories are created with restrictive permissions consistent with sandbox registry and global config patterns.
Missing factory store directories are treated as empty state for list operations.
Tests isolate the global config path with environment variables.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Persist and load factory run records
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want a run record to be saved and loaded by ID so that run state survives process restart.
acceptanceCriteria: Store API can create or save a run record with a stable run ID.
Store API can load a run record by run ID.
Loading a missing run returns an error compatible with errors.Is(..., fs.ErrNotExist).
Run record writes use temp-file plus rename atomic persistence.
Saved run records use restrictive file permissions consistent with global sandbox registry writes.
Tests verify a saved run can be loaded by a new store instance.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: List stored factory run records
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want stored run records to be listed deterministically so that factory history can be inspected after restart.
acceptanceCriteria: Store API can list all committed run records.
Runs are ordered newest-first by creation or update timestamp with a stable tie-breaker by run ID.
Missing factory directories return an empty list without creating malformed state.
Tests verify empty-state listing and deterministic ordering.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Append durable timeline events
userStories.description: As a factory user, I want timeline events to append durably so that lifecycle history remains available and ordered.
acceptanceCriteria: Store API can append an event to a run timeline without replacing previous events.
Store API can load events for a run in append order.
Supported event types include run creation, step start, step end, command output summary, verification result, CI state, artifact sync, and failure classification.
Timeline event writes are committed atomically.
Tests verify event ordering remains unchanged after creating a new store instance.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Ignore incomplete factory temp files
userStories.description: As a Hal maintainer, I want failed or leftover temp writes to avoid corrupting existing records or timelines.
acceptanceCriteria: Store read paths ignore incomplete temp files.
A leftover temp file does not replace or corrupt an existing run record.
A leftover temp file does not replace or corrupt an existing event timeline.
Tests create temp-file artifacts and verify previously committed run records still load correctly.
Tests create temp-file artifacts and verify previously committed timelines still load correctly.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add factory list JSON command
userStories.description: As an automation user, I want hal factory list --json to emit stored runs in deterministic order so that scripts can inspect factory history.
acceptanceCriteria: Add hal factory list --json.
JSON output includes an explicit documented contract version.
JSON output includes an array of run summaries without full event timelines.
Runs are emitted newest-first by creation or update timestamp with stable tie-breaking by run ID.
Empty state emits valid JSON with an empty runs array.
Command tests verify JSON keys, ordering, and empty-state behavior.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add factory status JSON command
userStories.description: As an automation user, I want hal factory status <run-id> --json to emit a run record and timeline so that one run can be inspected programmatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Add hal factory status <run-id> --json.
JSON output includes an explicit documented contract version.
JSON output includes the full run record and event timeline.
Timeline events preserve append order in command output.
Missing run IDs return a non-zero error with no malformed JSON payload.
Command tests verify JSON shape, ordered timeline output, and missing-run behavior.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Document factory JSON contracts
userStories.description: As an integrator, I want contract documentation and examples so that machine-readable factory output can be consumed safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Add contract documentation under docs/contracts/ for factory list, factory status, and timeline surfaces.
Add example JSON payloads for list output and status output with timeline events.
Documentation names required fields, optional fields, status values, event types, ordering rules, and error behavior.
Documentation states that existing .hal/prd.json, .hal/auto-state.json, and .hal/progress.txt contracts are unchanged.
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Lock factory JSON contracts with tests
userStories.description: As a Hal maintainer, I want contract-locking tests so that future changes do not accidentally break machine-readable factory output.
acceptanceCriteria: Add machine-contract tests that assert exact top-level JSON keys for factory list output.
Add machine-contract tests that assert exact top-level JSON keys for factory status output.
Add doc-code sync tests that verify factory JSON examples remain valid against the command output schema.
Add round-trip tests for run records, failure summaries, artifact references, and timeline events.
go test ./... passes
go vet ./... passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add factory command metadata coverage
userStories.description: As a Hal maintainer, I want command metadata tests for the factory command family so that generated CLI docs remain complete.
acceptanceCriteria: Factory command and subcommands include Use, Short, Long, and Example metadata.
Command metadata tests cover the factory command family.
Focused help tests assert required help phrases for factory list and factory status JSON usage.
README or generated CLI reference links remain consistent with the new command family when docs are generated.
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
